_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MS/ DNB/ PG Diploma or equivalent (in ENT)
clinical_indications: Nasal obstruction associated with external nasal deformity

preauth.label: Clinical notes (detailing signs, symptoms, examination findings, indications for doing the procedure, circumstances of the incident which led to disfigurement and advise for admission)
Clinical picture of the affected part along with full face of the patient and anterior rhinoscopy/ endoscopic picture showing deviated septum

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes (including clinical signs & symptoms such as difficulty in breathing, examination findings, indications for doing the procedure, Evidence of the circumstances of the incident which led to disfigurement& advise for admission)?
Clinical picture of the affected part along with full face of the patient and anterior rhinoscopy/ endoscopic picture showing deviated septum?

cpd_claim.q: Do OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed and outcomes of the surgery?
Are the documents available to show appropriate post-op care, advise including for follow-up?
Are post procedure photographs of the affected part available?

common_queries: Signs, symptoms, examination, or clinical pictures do not confirm the need for functional septorhinoplasty.
Evidence of other causes of breathing difficulty (other than nasal septum deformity) not ruled out.
Clinical notes are incomplete or missing required details (signs, symptoms, examination findings, indications, circumstances of incident, advise for admission).
Clinical picture of the affected part, full face of the patient, or anterior rhinoscopy/endoscopic picture showing deviated septum is missing or inadequate at pre-authorization.
Indoor case papers are missing at claim submission.
Operative/procedure notes are missing or lack detail on surgical steps and outcomes at claim submission.
Post-procedure photographs of the affected part are not available at claim submission.
Detailed discharge summary is missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination.
Functional septo-rhinoplasty is a procedure that is used to address nasal obstruction secondary to anatomic nasal deformities in the nasal septum and external nasal framework.
Indications: Nasal obstruction associated with external nasal deformity
Signs & Symptoms: nasal obstruction and external deformity like crooked nose, saddle nose etc.
contraindications: Surgery should not be planned in children before completion of the development of facial skeleton (atleast 15 to 16 years of age)
